nodes.name: Send Email: Send Email #1: Chat Follow-Up + Pre-Frame

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "What we didn't get to cover in our chat...", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
The one thing most South Florida homeowners don’t check until it’s too late. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

Thanks for chatting with us earlier. I know those conversations can feel rushed, so I wanted to follow up with something that didn’t come up.

There’s one detail about your home’s hurricane protection that determines everything — whether your insurance claim gets paid, whether your home passes inspection, and whether your family is actually safe when it matters.

Most homeowners in {{contact.city}} assume they’re covered. The data says otherwise.

Over the next couple of days, I’m going to share exactly what that detail is, why it matters more than the windows themselves, and what you can do about it — even if you’re not planning to make any changes right now.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send SMS: Send SMS #1: Standalone Emotional Hook

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'SMS', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, message: "Hey {{contact.first_name}}, quick question, do you know what rating your windows have? It's the #1 thing insurers check after a storm, and most homeowners have no idea.", fromNumber: "+19542808890" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: Send Email #2: Belief Shift - The Hidden Vulnerabi

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "The part your insurer checks first (it's not what you think)", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
A homeowner in Tampa found out the hard way last hurricane season. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

Last hurricane season, a homeowner in Tampa filed a $45,000 wind damage claim. Impact windows installed. Shutters on every opening. They did everything right — or so they thought.

The claim was denied.

The reason? Their windows weren’t rated for the wind zone they actually lived in. The previous contractor installed windows that met code for a different zone — one with lower wind speed requirements. On paper, they had impact windows. In reality, they had a gap that voided their coverage.

This isn’t rare. It’s more common than most homeowners in South Florida realize.

The distinction that matters isn’t whether you have impact windows, it’s whether your windows carry the correct certification for YOUR specific wind zone, YOUR building code jurisdiction, and YOUR insurance carrier’s requirements.

Tomorrow, I’ll share exactly how to verify this yourself in about 5 minutes — no appointment needed, no sales pitch.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.address}}, {{location.city}}, {{location.state}} {{location.postal_code}}
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}
